pages.raw_text:
The question of the danger to
which 800 little tots who attend
the Whittier school are at present
exposed on account of no water
connections and lack of proper
escapes in case of fire, will be
taken up at a meeting of the
school board in the administration
building tonight


But Secretary Thomas this
morning stated that the board
would probably decide not to make
a water connection on account of
danger of freezing during the cold
weather, and that no water would
probably be run into the school
until a hot water plant Is put IN.
The children now drink out of a
bucket, one cup doing service for
most of them, it is claimed.


Recently the fire commission
condemned the old furnace, which
had charred the floor above It on
account of it's defects, and this will
not be used again, it is stated.
Stoves in each room will be SO
arranged as to afford protection
from fire, according to Mr.
Thomas. Stairway escapes are to
be installed on two sides of the
building.


Residents of West Grove add
ton, whose children attend the
school, momentarily expect a repe
tition of the Collingwood horror
and are urging the school beard to
hasten the work of installing fire
escapes. At present, it is explain
ed, there is danger of ire only q
part of the day as the stoves are
not kept going all the time. 1t is
expected that before the weather
turns so cold that fires must be
maintained throughout the day
that the escapes will be in place.


The fact that a fire could start
in 10 minutes seem to be over
looked. The board is again con
victed of a false sense of security
in regard to the fire danger.


The city has fire commission to
look out for such things, but the
board appear to be paramount t.
its authority.
